summary: Polysulfides have inferior long-term storage capabilities due primarily to their rapid absorption of atmospheric humidity causing physical degradation; thus they exhibit significantly reduced durability beyond immediate use relative to silicones and polyethers known for better resistance against environmental factors affecting longevity during extended periods between packaging opening and application completion stages necessary within dental practice settings where precision is paramount throughout each procedure cycle involving new material dispensings as needed per patient needs
